description: Chapter 2.6.3 - Favorite Artists Premium Gallery Experience. Transform the artists tab in the favorites page into a luxury gallery with hero section, statistics, search/filter/sort, premium artist cards (5-col grid), artist details drawer, similar artists, recommendations, and empty/loading/error states. All client-side using existing FavoritesContext/PersonalizationContext + localStorage.
steps: Create app/components/favorites/artists/ directory for all new components

Create FavoriteArtistsHero.tsx - Glass Level 5 hero with gradient from artist portraits, favorite count, genres represented, top artist, recently added artist, animated background using color extraction, quick action buttons (Browse All, Discover Similar, Search, Sort, Filter) with Glass Level 3 + hover-glow
Create ArtistStatistics.tsx - Animated counters (700ms) showing: Favorite Artists count, Albums Saved, Songs Saved, Genres, using glass-subtle cards
Create ArtistSearch.tsx - Instant search with 150ms debounce, supports artist name and genre filtering, uses glass-search styling
Create ArtistFilters.tsx - Multi-select filters for Genre, Popularity, Verified (popularity >= 70), Recently Added. Use glass-medium buttons with active state
Create ArtistSorting.tsx - Sort by Recently Added (default), Alphabetical, Popularity. Dropdown with glass-dropdown styling

Create FavoriteArtistCard.tsx - Premium gallery card with: portrait (rounded), verified badge (BadgeCheck from lucide), artist name, genre, popularity indicator, saved date, favorite button, share button. 180ms hover with portrait zoom, glass reflection, glow border, ambient shadow. Glass Level 3. Cards maintain consistent aspect ratio in 5-col (desktop), 3-col (tablet), 2-col (mobile) grid

Create ArtistDetailsDrawer.tsx - Slide-out drawer (Glass Level 5) that opens without navigation. Contains: large portrait, biography (short with Read More), genres, followers, popularity, latest release, top songs preview (max 10), albums carousel, similar artists, external links (Spotify, Last.fm). Fetch artist data from existing API routes /api/spotify/artist when drawer opens
Create TopSongsPreview.tsx (reusable within drawer) - Shows up to 10 songs with artwork, name, album, duration, popularity bar, play preview button
Create DiscographyPreview.tsx (reusable within drawer) - Horizontal carousel of albums/singles with artwork, title, year, track count
Create SimilarArtistsPanel.tsx - Shows similar artists with portrait, name, genres, recommendation reason, favorite button
Create ArtistRecommendations.tsx - Discovery section based on favorite artists' genres, shows recommended artists with reason always visible
Create FavoriteArtistsSkeleton.tsx - Progressive loading skeleton with shimmer-wave for hero, statistics, and card grid
Create EmptyFavoriteArtists.tsx - Premium empty state with illustration, message, and buttons (Discover Artists, Trending Artists, Browse Genres)
Update app/favorites/page.tsx - Replace the simple artists tab content with the new FavoriteArtistsPage component that orchestrates all sub-components
Ensure full keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus-visible indicators, semantic HTML (section, nav, article elements), touch targets >= 44px
Add prefers-reduced-motion support - all animations respect the system preference
Responsive: desktop 5-col grid, tablet 3-col, mobile 2-col. Drawer becomes bottom sheet on mobile
acceptance_criteria: Artists tab renders premium hero with dynamic gradient and glass overlay
Artist statistics display with animated counters
Search filters artists in real-time with < 80ms perceived response
Filter chips allow multi-select genre/popularity filtering
Artist cards display portrait, name, genre, badges in responsive grid
Hovering cards shows glow, lift, and portrait zoom animations
Clicking an artist card opens the details drawer without navigation
Details drawer shows biography, top songs, discography, similar artists
Empty state renders when no favorite artists exist
Loading skeleton appears during initial hydration
All components pass keyboard navigation and screen reader testing
bun run lint passes with zero errors
bun run build succeeds with zero errors
verification: Run `bun run lint` and confirm zero errors
Run `bun run build` and confirm successful compilation
findings: 1. React 19 strict ESLint rules: setState inside useEffect flagged by react-hooks/set-state-in-effect - resolved by using useSyncExternalStore for hydration detection and rAF-based animation in AnimatedCounter. 2. react-hooks/purity rule prevents Math.random and Date.now calls inside component bodies even in event handlers declared inline - resolved with deterministic hash-based IDs. 3. @next/next/no-html-link-for-pages requires Link from next/link for all internal routes including query-string-based ones. 4. The /api/spotify/artist response structure includes artist.topTracks, artist.albums, artist.relatedArtists based on getSpotifyArtistDetails. 5. animate-slide-in-right already defined in globals.css for drawer animation.
